author: gb9t
content: Iteration 8: broadcom/bnxt.md committed (8767e7b, 256 lines). Largest single-driver target so far at ~34000 upstream lines: BCM5731x/5741x/5750x(Thor)/5760x(Thor2)/5770x. Headline architecture: HWRM (versioned 10K-line auto-gen FW ABI, hundreds of opcodes). Rust translation: HwrmReq<'_, T> RAII guard makes use-after-free impossible at compile time (sentinel becomes runtime backup), bilge bit-packed for hsi.h structs, build.rs auto-gen from upstream spec. Grsec: HWRM as primary attack surface — response length per-opcode validation, opcode allowlist per-VF (closes the historical 'VF escapes to PF via HWRM' CVE class), FW image signature mandatory on install. SR-IOV + switchdev + TC offload (CFA) + RoCE-ULP-aux-bus + DCB + PTP + coredump all covered. Cloud-perimeter coverage: ENA+gVNIC+MANA+bnxt now complete. Next iteration target: drivers/nvme/host/pci.c — NVMe-PCIe driver (storage tier — every modern Linux SSD/NVMe host).
